title: simulation.py: negative jitter_min_us not validated, can cause past-time events
description:
File: python/src/lichen/sim/simulation.py
Lines: 101-106, 169-178

Simulation.__init__ validates that jitter_min_us <= jitter_max_us, but does not validate that jitter_min_us is non-negative.

If jitter_min_us is negative, calculate_tx_jitter() can return negative jitter values, causing TxStartDelayedEvent to be scheduled in the past:
```python
delayed_event = TxStartDelayedEvent(
    time_us=self._current_time_us + jitter,  # jitter could be negative
    ...
)
```

Events scheduled in the past will fire immediately, but this semantic is confusing and likely unintended by callers.

Failure scenario:
```python
sim = Simulation("test", jitter_min_us=-5000, jitter_max_us=5000)
# calculate_tx_jitter() may return -3000
# TX event scheduled at current_time - 3000us (in the past)
# Event fires immediately, causing non-intuitive timing behavior
```

Fix: Add validation that jitter_min_us >= 0:
```python
if jitter_min_us < 0:
    raise ValueError(f"jitter_min_us must be non-negative, got {jitter_min_us}")
```
close_reason: Added regression test for negative jitter validation in Simulation.__init__ (validation was already present in code). Test passes, ruff clean.
